> **Historical generated audit output, not the current roadmap (2026-07-09):** This June 23 synthesis is preserved for institutional-readiness reasoning. Later pipeline fixes, Desktop/local-first work, and documentation sweeps may change the priority order or status of individual items. Verify against current source/tests and current docs before treating any direction as open.

# AlloFlow — Next Directions (Institutional-Readiness Track)

*Read-only synthesis from the five subsystem maps + 24 confirmed findings. Ordered by UMaine-review credibility leverage, not by effort.*

---

## 0. The through-line that ties almost every HIGH together

Four of the five "HIGH" findings are the **same defect class in different surfaces: content fidelity is measured by *magnitude*, never by *order*.**

- HTML fix loop accepts on length/char-count floors (`aiFixChunked` :2950) and joins chunks blindly (`fixed.join('')` :3030).
- Tagged-PDF post-save check is a `new Set(tokens)` membership test (:20786) — order-insensitive by construction.
- Stage-4 pairs segments↔items by pure index (:6203) on equal counts, assuming two differently-ordered lists agree.
- The 97% coverage net (:16683) is a character count that *strips the recovery appendix first*.

The genuine fix already exists and is exported: **`checkReadingOrderPreserved` (:509)** — but it is wired only into the three *safest* paths (S3 block-restyle via `_restyleGate` :601). For a reviewer like Garry or Paul Cochrane, "we ship a PDF/UA-1 declaration on a document whose reading order we never verified" is the single most disqualifying sentence that can be written about this tool. **This is the spine of directions D1–D2 below, and it is why they are ranked first.**

---

## Top strategic directions for institutional readiness

### D1 — Make reading order a first-class, order-sensitive gate (HTML path). **[Highest leverage]**
Call the *existing* `checkReadingOrderPreserved(prevHtml, fixedHtml)` inside `acceptFixedHtml` and in `aiFixChunked`'s per-chunk accept path; on `ok===false`, revert/keep-original or raise a reading-order WARN. This converts a "we hope the model didn't reorder" posture into "we verified it didn't," using a function that's already written, tested, and exported. Pair with the chunk-splitting fix (split only at top-level block boundaries; never inside `<table>/<ul>/<figure>`; tag-balance + cell-count assertion on each join) so the order check operates on structurally-valid input. Small code, enormous credibility delta.

### D2 — Order-sensitive tagged-PDF verification + multi-column golden. **[Highest leverage]**
Replace the set-membership round-trip with an **LCS/sequence ratio** of the deterministic reading-order token *sequence* vs the assembled tag-tree leaf *sequence*; withhold `pdfuaid:part=1` and raise a banner on divergence. In `_stage4_matchSegmentsToItems`, validate that index-paired items are monotonic in (y,x) before trusting the equal-count fast path; otherwise fall to nearest-neighbour. **Pin a two-column golden fixture** — the strategic audit already flags that goldens are single-column page-1 synthetics, which is exactly why this whole class survives every gate. Also fold AcroForm/Link StructElems into per-page (y,x) position rather than concatenating them as trailing siblings, and add `Form` to `_COUNTABLE` (finding #17) — fillable IEPs/permission slips are the literal target document and currently read "all prose, then all fields."

### D3 — "The score must describe the bytes you're shipping." Close the measurement-honesty concurrency + revert gaps.
Three findings are one theme: a displayed score that attests to a *different* document than the stored one.
- `_reauditAndScore` (:2850) has no run-generation token — a second edit mid-audit makes the headline describe stale bytes. Capture `newHtml`/an edit token at entry and bail in the terminal `setPdfFixResult` when `prev.accessibleHtml !== newHtml`, mirroring `runAutoFixLoop`'s `_myRunGen`.
- Workbench "Revert last command" (:10258) restores HTML but never re-scores; `_revertPalette` (:3237) *does*. Make them consistent (call `_reauditAndScore` or set a `_scoreStale` flag).
- A partial *final* audit (1/6 chunks lost, < 25% NULL threshold) governs the headline with no asterisk. Propagate `_partialAudit` onto the result object and either mark it or treat a partial *final authoritative* audit as `_aiVerificationIncomplete`.

These are individually small but collectively define whether the headline number is defensible under questioning.

### D4 — Stop the cross-document state bleed (one-session reliability). **[Directly addresses "doesn't work every time" reports]**
Two findings share a root cause and a root fix: the results-screen **Load Project never unmounts `PdfAuditView`** (no `key` prop, `pdfAuditResult` left truthy), so refs survive into the next document — palette Revert can overwrite doc B with doc A's HTML, and stale OCR window globals (`__lastOcrTesseractText`/`__lastOcrVisionText`/`__lastOcrMethod`) leak the prior scanned doc's words into the next born-digital doc's AutoRestore and mislabel it "scanned." **Fix:** give `PdfAuditView` a `key` tied to document identity (forces remount = clean slate, the design's own assumed contract), and reset the four OCR globals in the Step-1 reset block (:13448) alongside the others. Also destroy the leaked image-extraction `pdfDoc` in a `finally` (:14067) — the only `getDocument` site that doesn't. These three together are the most likely cause of multi-doc-session failures.

### D5 — Honest alt-text and honest "Verified" labels (overclaim-by-presence). **[Pure credibility, low effort]**
- Deterministic alt fallback stamps `alt='Document image'`/`'Image'` (:15775) which clears axe `image-alt` by *presence* while giving a screen-reader user nothing — yet the surgical path already knows the honest pattern (`'Image (needs description)'` :12062). Make the fallback honest + flag generic-alt as unresolved in the content audit.
- `'Analysis + Verified'` (:2372) brands a Gemini *self-reported* search check as independent verification. Relabel to "AI fact-check (not independently verified)" and carry the bibliography caveat.
- The `generateBibliographyString` honesty hardening landed only in `content_engine`'s local copy; the **shared `text_pipeline_helpers` copy (:321) still defaults to `'Verified Sources'` with no caveat**, and the live dispatcher delegates to it. Port the caveat to the shared module — this is the residual the prior review flagged as "not fully fixed."

### D6 — Capture live typing before any persistence/export reads `accessibleHtml`.
Plain `designMode` typing and the Ctrl+heading/link/list `execCommand`s fire no sync signal, so Save & Close and HTML/ODT/DAISY/EPUB silently drop them. **Fix:** harvest `getPdfPreviewHtml()` before any path that reads `accessibleHtml` directly (the `:11008` pattern), or bind an `input`/`blur` listener that calls the debounced `__alloflowOnPdfPreviewMutated`. This is data-loss on the teacher's own corrections — high trust cost, modest fix.

### D7 — Build the calibration corpus (the unglamorous prerequisite). **[Strategic, not a code fix]**
The min()-of-two-layers method is principled but, per the strategic audit + validation plan, **its agreement with expert/PAC/veraPDF judgment is unvalidated** and the corpus is empty. No amount of in-app honesty copy substitutes for "here are N documents scored by AlloFlow and by a human PAC reviewer, and here is the agreement." This is the artifact a UMaine gatekeeper will actually ask for. Recommend a small (20–40 doc) hand-scored corpus spanning the hard cases the findings expose: multi-column, transposed tables, scanned IEPs, figure-heavy, RTL.

---

## Explicit disposition of the three previously-suggested ideas

### (1) More vetted block-restyle transforms (definition→sidebar, stat→pull-quote-without-duplication) — **DEFER**
The block-restyle path is the *one* place the architecture already does this right: `_restyleGate` (:600) gates every transform through `checkReadingOrderPreserved` (:509), and the region/restyle machinery was just hardened (b0d24ae3 scar work, `_spliceBlock` single-occurrence safety, ambiguity refusal). So new transforms are *architecturally cheap and safe to add*. But two of the four HIGH findings (D1, D2) prove the **same order-verification discipline is missing from the high-volume paths that actually ship most documents** (the AI fix loop and the tagged-PDF tree). Adding more transforms to the already-safe 1% while the 99% remains order-blind is polishing the wrong surface for an institutional review. **Defer until D1 is done** — once `checkReadingOrderPreserved` guards the fix loop, new transforms ride proven infrastructure and "stat→pull-quote-without-duplication" specifically becomes a natural test case for the order/dedup guard. Good idea, wrong sequence.

### (2) Profile-aware engagement defaults (suppress engagement features for low-load UDL profiles) — **DROP** (as currently scoped)
This is a real UDL instinct, but it is mis-targeted for this codebase at this moment. The maps show AlloFlow's "engagement" surface is the *Document-Builder generation side* (resource types, leveled text, companions) — and that side's open debts are **honesty debts, not load-tuning debts**: ungrounded-by-default generation, the `'Verified'`/`'Verified Sources'` overclaims (findings #19, and the divergent-copy risk), positional-not-semantic citation binding. A "suppress for low-load profiles" feature adds a new configuration axis (and new state to leak across documents — see D4, which shows the component *already* mishandles cross-document state) without retiring any of those debts. It also presumes a profile-classification the tool doesn't have and that a school psychologist would (rightly) want evidence behind before it silently changes what a student sees. **Drop the "suppress" framing.** If the underlying goal (don't overwhelm a low-load learner) is wanted, the honest version is the *existing leveled-text + plain-language companion* path — and the higher-value work there is fixing the **length-only companion staleness check** (finding #12: an equal-length edit leaves a stale translation/plain-language companion presented as current, surviving save/load — an equity hazard for a translated IEP). Redirect the energy there.

### (3) Voice / STT into the intent fields — **DROP**
The intent fields feed the scoped-AI-intent and region-restyle edit paths. Those paths are exactly where the maps surface unresolved correctness hazards: `_reauditAndScore` has no stale-HTML guard (D3), the region-select live↔stored bridge maps by document-order position and silently no-ops on `_spliceBlock` not-found (region map risk), and edits route raw document HTML into rewrite prompts. Adding a *new, lower-precision input modality* (STT mis-transcription of an intent like "make this a heading level two" → "make this heading too") on top of edit plumbing that already has concurrency and silent-no-op gaps increases the surface for "I told it to do X and it did Y / nothing." There is also no evidence in any map of a user request for it, no a11y gap it uniquely closes (the keyboard `execCommand` shortcuts and the typed intent field already cover the operations), and the FERPA map notes TTS already has a local-WASM fallback story — STT would (re)open a new egress/permission question (mic capture) for marginal benefit. **Drop.** If voice accessibility for the *operator* is genuinely wanted, the OS/browser dictation already types into the existing text field for free; build nothing.

---

## One-line priority stack
**D1 + D2 (reading-order gates, the spine) → D4 (cross-document bleed / session reliability) → D3 (score-describes-shipped-bytes) → D5 + D6 (overclaim labels + live-edit capture) → D7 (calibration corpus, the artifact the review will demand).** Idea (1) **DEFER** behind D1; ideas (2) and (3) **DROP**.
